mission.title: istio: Regex rewrites using capture groups
mission.description: Regex rewrites using capture groups. This issue affects 131+ users.

steps.title: Identify istio feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your istio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=istio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Regex rewrites using capture groups

steps.title: Check current Ingress configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio resources:
```bash
kubectl get ingress -A
kubectl describe ingress <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Describe the feature request**
Given that Envoy seems to already support [regex rewrites](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v2/api/v2/route/route_components.proto#envoy-api-field-route

steps.title: Apply the fix for Regex rewrites using capture groups
steps.description:
Tickets: https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/22290

```yaml
I tested it with httpbin.  Requests for `httpbin:8000/banana/418` were processed as `httpbin:8000/banana/418`.

What do you think about me making a sample or blog about this for Istio?

If people find the performance of doing this with WASM reasonable, we could talk about smoother experience for users who want path rewrites without learning the EnvoyFilter API.
I do think having single wasm examples is useful but afaik this is supported in envoy already (https://github.com/istio/api/pull/1854)
> @istio/wg-policies-and-telemetry-maintainers This is a common request, as is rewriting the HTTP
```

steps.title: Confirm Regex rewrites using capture groups is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

codeSnippets:
I tested it with httpbin.  Requests for `httpbin:8000/banana/418` were processed as `httpbin:8000/banana/418`.

What do you think about me making a sample or blog about this for Istio?

If people find the performance of doing this with WASM reasonable, we could talk about smoother experience for users who want path rewrites without learning the EnvoyFilter API.
I do think having single wasm examples is useful but afaik this is supported in envoy already (https://github.com/istio/api/pull/1854)
> @istio/wg-policies-and-telemetry-maintainers This is a common request, as is rewriting the HTTP path based on a regex. I don't think Envoy includes support for such rewrites. This might make a good example of using Wasm. I created a filter to do this and it is very simple. The non-boilerplate i

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
